title: a thought of mine
wikitext:
[]: a thought of mine...             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Wed Jun 15, 2005 10:01 pm pardon any n00bishness i shall spout henceforth. i was bored and decided to peruse the images of the evangelion episode screenplays scanned in by thekillingmantra and hosted at Rei liberation parade. i had been wondering about the significance of episode 2's english title, "the beast," and i found something interesting. suppose you add the number of cuts in episodes 1 and 2 together. the screenplay of episode 1 has 351 numbered cuts. subtract 351 from 666, the widely alleged "number of the beast," and you get 315. cut #315 in episode 2's screenplay is the one in which ritsuko (although it's Misato in the finished episode) says "hontou no sugata" ("the true identity [of eva]"). could it be that eva is "the beast"? i realize this is probably nothing more than a completely absurd coincidence, but what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 15, 2005 11:55 pm Japanese fans have connected "The Beast" with the Anti-Christ. I make some comments about the matter in the commentary, episode #02, scene 5.

[]: Congratulations!             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Jun 16, 2005 12:09 am

[]: Re: a thought of mine...             Quote Post by Prons » Thu Jun 16, 2005 9:30 am toiletduck wrote: pardon any n00bishness i shall spout henceforth. i was bored and decided to peruse the images of the evangelion episode screenplays scanned in by thekillingmantra and hosted at Rei liberation parade. i had been wondering about the significance of episode 2's english title, "the beast," and i found something interesting. suppose you add the number of cuts in episodes 1 and 2 together. the screenplay of episode 1 has 351 numbered cuts. subtract 351 from 666, the widely alleged "number of the beast," and you get 315. cut #315 in episode 2's screenplay is the one in which ritsuko (although it's Misato in the finished episode) says "hontou no sugata" ("the true identity [of eva]"). could it be that eva is "the beast"? i realize this is probably nothing more than a completely absurd coincidence, but what do you guys think?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Jun 16, 2005 9:40 am The dark side of the maths, numerology is.

[]:             Quote Post by Prons » Thu Jun 16, 2005 9:42 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: The dark side of the maths, numerology is.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Jun 16, 2005 9:47 am Nahh, Prons, you aren't the only one. I doubt it a lot. I seriously think numerology (is that the right term?) is just funny. You can prove ANYTHING with numberology. If you want, I can prove you're me. It's easy. You just need to find the right "significant numbers" to multiply and divide by and add and subtract from the starting number. Since whoever is doing the numerology gets to be the one who pick ALL of these numbers, it's easy to work out any answer you like. Seriously, give me a few hours, and I'll prove that Eva Monkey is Hideaki Anno. Actually, I won't because I'm not that bored, but I could. I god damn guarantee it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:03 am Well, sometimes numbers have some significance... unless you're going to suggest that the seven eyes on Seele's mark or the twelve wings of GNR/Sho were just numbers that the Gainax boys randomly picked.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:07 am umm perhaps i'm just stupid, but what was the significance of the numbers of eyes and wings (perhaps the months?) or perhaps you could direct me to a thread where it is discussed? and i agree, numerology isnt really accurate...at all

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:17 am sadsadshinji wrote: umm perhaps i'm just stupid, but what was the significance of the numbers of eyes and wings (perhaps the months?) or perhaps you could direct me to a thread where it is discussed? and i agree, numerology isnt really accurate...at all

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:21 am thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Jun 16, 2005 2:24 pm It's reportedly 616, so either you lose or Anno does. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Oroboro » Thu Jun 16, 2005 3:22 pm I know the that 7 happens to be God's favorite number, thus accounting for the eyes. I think the angel Michael, or was Gaberiel, has twelve wings. Anyhoo, what posessed you to count the number of cuts?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Jun 16, 2005 5:12 pm Reichu wrote: Well, sometimes numbers have some significance... unless you're going to suggest that the seven eyes on Seele's mark or the twelve wings of GNR/Sho were just numbers that the Gainax boys randomly picked.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Jun 16, 2005 6:18 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: It's reportedly 616, so either you lose or Anno does. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Jun 16, 2005 10:37 pm What comes out? Nero?

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Thu Jun 16, 2005 11:05 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: What comes out? Nero?

[]:             Quote Post by Prons » Fri Jun 17, 2005 6:43 am Reichu wrote: Well, sometimes numbers have some significance... unless you're going to suggest that the seven eyes on Seele's mark or the twelve wings of GNR/Sho were just numbers that the Gainax boys randomly picked.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jun 17, 2005 9:12 am Oroboro wrote: I know the that 7 happens to be God's favorite number, thus accounting for the eyes. I think the angel Michael, or was Gaberiel, has twelve wings.

[]:             Quote Post by Oroboro » Fri Jun 17, 2005 1:37 pm Reichu wrote: Oroboro wrote: I know the that 7 happens to be God's favorite number, thus accounting for the eyes. I think the angel Michael, or was Gaberiel, has twelve wings.
